1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: DESTROYED WILL BE the hands of Abu Lahab, and he himself will perish.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: May both the hands of Abu Lahab be destroyed – and they are destroyed!

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Perish the two hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Perish the two hands of Abu Lahab (an uncle of the Prophet), and perish he!

1.author: Itani
1.text: Condemned are the hands of Abee Lahab, and he is condemned.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Destroyed were the hands of Abu Lahab, and he lay utterly doomed.

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Perish the two hands of Abu Lahab and perish he!

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: The power of Abu Lahab will perish, and he will perish.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Perish the hands of AbiLahab, and perish he!

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: May the hands of Abu Lahab be ruined, and ruined is he.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: May the hands of Abu Lahab perish!

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Perdition overtake both hands of Abu Lahab, and he will perish.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: May the hands of Abu Lahab perish, may he be ruined.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Perish the hands of the Father of Flame! Perish he!

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Of no avail shall be his wealth, nor what he has acquired.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: His wealth did not benefit him in the least, nor did whatever he earned.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: His wealth avails him not, neither what he has earned;

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: His substance availed him not, nor that which he earned.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: His wealth and his children (etc.) will not benefit him!

2.author: Itani
2.text: His wealth did not avail him, nor did what he acquired.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: His wealth did not avail him, nor his acquisitions.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: His wealth and his children will not benefit him!

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: His wealth and gains will not exempt him.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Neither his wealth availed him, nor what he had earned.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: His wealth will not suffice him, neither what he has gained;

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: His wealth will not avail him or that which he gained.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: His wealth and what he earns will not avail him.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Neither his wealth nor his gains will avail him.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: No profit to him from all his wealth, and all his gains!

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Ma aghna AAanhu maluhu wama kasaba

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: He will be roasted in the fire,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: He will soon enter the flaming fire.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: he shall roast at a flaming fire

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Anon he shall roast into Fire having flame.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: He will be burnt in a Fire of blazing flames!

3.author: Itani
3.text: He will burn in a Flaming Fire.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Surely, he will be cast into a Flaming Fire

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: He will enter a Fire full of flames!

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: He will be plunged in flaming Fire,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Soon he will enter the blazing fire,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: he shall roast at a Flaming Fire,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: He will [enter to] burn in a Fire of [blazing] flame

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: His property and worldly gains will be of no help to him.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: He shall soon burn in fire that flames,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: He shall soon enter a Blazing Fire,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Burnt soon will he be in a Fire of Blazing Flame!

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: And his wife, the portress of fire wood,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And so will his wife; carrying a bundle of firewood on her head.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: and his wife, the carrier of the firewood,

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And his wife also: the firewood carrier;

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And his wife too, who carries wood (thorns of Sadan which she used to put on the way of the Prophet (Peace be upon him), or use to slander him).

4.author: Maududi
4.text: along with his wife, that carrier of slanderous tales;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And his wife too, who carries wood.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: and his wife [too], the firewood carrier,

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: and his wife, laden with firewood

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And his wife [as well] - the carrier of firewood.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: He will suffer in a blazing fire

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And his wife, the bearer of fuel,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: and also his wife who carries the fuel,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: His wife shall carry the (crackling) wood - As fuel!-

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Will have a strap of fibre rope around her neck.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: A rope made from palm fibre around her neck!

5.author: Arberry
5.text: upon her neck a rope of palm-fibre.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: On her neck shall be a cord of twisted fibre.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: In her neck is a twisted rope of Masad (palm fibre).

5.author: Itani
5.text: Around her neck is a rope of thorns.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: upon her neck shall be a rope of palm-fibre.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: In her neck is a twisted rope of Masad.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Will have upon her neck a halter of palm-fibre.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: with a rope of palm fibre around her neck.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: shall have a rope of palmfiber around her neck!

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Around her neck is a rope of [twisted] fiber.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: and so too will his wife who (threw thorns and firewood in the Prophet's way). Around her neck will be a rope of palm fibre.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Upon her neck a halter of strongly twisted rope.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: with a rope of twisted fibre round her neck.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: A twisted rope of palm-leaf fibre round her (own) neck!
